var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. args.code:
import json
import re

# Load data from storage
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:48']

# Create mapping from business_id to business info
business_map = {}
for biz in business_data:
    business_id = biz['business_id']  # e.g., "businessid_49"
    business_map[business_id] = biz

# Function to extract categories from description
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z"
    patterns = [
        r'providing a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a wide range of services, including ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)',
        r'including ([^.]+)',
        r'services in ([^.]+)'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories if cat.strip()]
            # Remove trailing period
            categories = [cat.rstrip('.') for cat in categories]
            return categories
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_reviews = 0

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']  # e.g., "businessref_36"
    # Convert business_ref to business_id format
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            matched_reviews += 1
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            # If no categories extracted, use the business name as category
            category = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
            matched_reviews += 1
    else:
        unmatched_reviews += 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

result = {
    "total_reviews": len(reviews_data),
    "matched_reviews": matched_reviews,
    "unmatched_reviews": unmatched_reviews,
    "top_categories": top_categories
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            # If no categories extracted, use the business name as category
            category = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
            matched_reviews += 1
    else:
        unmatched_reviews += 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

result = {
    "total_reviews": len(reviews_data),
    "matched_reviews": matched_reviews,
    "unmatched_reviews": unmatched_reviews,
    "top_categories": top_categories
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
import re

# Load data from storage
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:54']

# Create mapping from business_id to business info
business_map = {}
for biz in business_data:
    business_id = biz['business_id']  # e.g., "businessid_49"
    business_map[business_id] = biz

# Function to extract categories from description - IMPROVED
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, Z"
    patterns = [
        r'providing a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a wide range of services, including ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)',
        r'including ([^.]+?)(?: for |\.|$)',
        r'services in ([^.]+)',
        r'category of [\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            # Clean up each category
            cleaned = []
            for cat in categories:
                cat = cat.strip()
                # Remove leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation
                cat = cat.strip('.')
                cat = cat.strip()
                if cat and cat not in ['', ' ']:
                    # Skip if it's just "and" or similar
                    if cat.lower() not in ['', ' ']:
                        cleaned.append(cat)
            return cleaned
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_reviews = 0

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']  # e.g., "businessref_36"
    # Convert business_ref to business_id format
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            matched_reviews += 1
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            # If no categories extracted, use the business name or a default
            business_name = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            # Try to extract category from name if it contains common patterns
            matched = False
            for biz_type in ['Restaurant', 'Salon', 'School', 'Medical', 'Gym', 'Shop', 'Store']:
                if biz_type.lower() in business_name.lower():
                    category_review_count[biz_type] = category_review_count.get(biz_type, 0) + 1
                    matched = True

import json
import re

# Load data from storage
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:54']

# Create mapping from business_id to business info
business_map = {}
for biz in business_data:
    business_id = biz['business_id']  # e.g., "businessid_49"
    business_map[business_id] = biz

# Function to extract categories from description - IMPROVED
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, Z"
    patterns = [
        r'providing a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a wide range of services, including ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)',
        r'including ([^.]+?)(?: for |\.|$)',
        r'services in ([^.]+)',
        r'category of [\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            # Clean up each category
            cleaned = []
            for cat in categories:
                cat = cat.strip()
                # Remove leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation
                cat = cat.strip('.')
                cat = cat.strip()
                if cat and cat not in ['', ' ']:
                    # Skip if it's just "and" or similar
                    if cat.lower() not in ['', ' ']:
                        cleaned.append(cat)
            return cleaned
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_reviews = 0

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']  # e.g., "businessref_36"
    # Convert business_ref to business_id format
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            matched_reviews += 1
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            # If no categories extracted, use the business name or a default
            business_name = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            # Try to extract category from name if it contains common patterns
            matched = False
            for biz_type in ['Restaurant', 'Salon', 'School', 'Medical', 'Gym', 'Shop', 'Store']:
                if biz_type.lower() in business_name.lower():
                    category_review_count[biz_type] = category_review_count.get(biz_type, 0) + 1
                    matched = True
                    matched_reviews += 1
                    break
            if not matched:
                category_review_count[business_name] = category_review_count.get(business_name, 0) + 1
                matched_reviews += 1
    else:
        unmatched_reviews += 1

# Get top 10 categories to see more data
top_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]

result = {
    "total_reviews": len(reviews_data),
    "matched_reviews": matched_reviews,
    "unmatched_reviews": unmatched_reviews,
    "top_categories": top_categories,
    "all_categories_count": len(category_review_count)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json
import re

# Load data from storage
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:54']

# Create mapping from business_id to business info
business_map = {}
for biz in business_data:
    business_id = biz['business_id']  # e.g., "businessid_49"
    business_map[business_id] = biz

# Function to extract categories from description - IMPROVED
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, Z"
    patterns = [
        r'providing a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a wide range of services, including ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)',
        r'including ([^.]+?)(?: for |\.|$)',
        r'services in ([^.]+)',
        r'category of [\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            # Clean up each category
            cleaned = []
            for cat in categories:
                cat = cat.strip()
                # Remove leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation
                cat = cat.strip('.')
                cat = cat.strip()
                if cat and cat not in ['', ' ']:
                    if cat.lower() not in ['']:
                        cleaned.append(cat)
            return cleaned
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_reviews = 0

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']  # e.g., "businessref_36"
    # Convert business_ref to business_id format
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            matched_reviews += 1
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            # If no categories extracted, use the business name or a default
            business_name = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            # Try to extract category from name if it contains common patterns
            matched = False
            for biz_type in ['Restaurant', 'Salon', 'School', 'Medical', 'Gym', 'Shop', 'Store']:
                if biz_type.lower() in business_name.lower():
                    category_review_count[biz_type] = category_review_count.get(biz_type, 0) + 1
                    matched = True
                    matched_reviews += 1
                    break
            if not matched:
                category_review_count[business_name] = category_review_count.get(business_name, 0) + 1
                matched_reviews += 1
    else:
        unmatched_reviews += 1

# Get top 10 categories to see more data
top_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]

result = {
    "total_reviews": len(reviews_data),
    "matched_reviews": matched_reviews,
    "unmatched_reviews": unmatched_reviews,
    "top_categories": top_categories,
    "all_categories_count": len(category_review_count)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json
import re

# Load data from storage
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:54']

# Create mapping from business_id to business info
business_map = {}
for biz in business_data:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    business_map[business_id] = biz

# Function to extract categories from description
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z"
    patterns = [
        r'providing a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a wide range of services, including ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)',
        r'including ([^.]+?)(?: for |\.|$)',
        r'services in ([^.]+)'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            # Clean up each category
            cleaned = []
            for cat in categories:
                cat = cat.strip()
                # Remove leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation
                cat = cat.strip('.')
                cat = cat.strip()
                if cat and len(cat) > 0:
                    cleaned.append(cat)
            return cleaned
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_reviews = 0
business_matches = {}

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    # Convert business_ref to business_id format
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            matched_reviews += 1
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        else:
            business_name = business.get('name', 'Unknown')
            category_review_count[business_name] = category_review_count.get(business_name, 0) + 1
            matched_reviews += 1
    else:
        unmatched_reviews += 1

# Get top 20 categories to see the data
top_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:20]

result = {
    "total_reviews": len(reviews_data),
    "matched_reviews": matched_reviews,
    "unmatched_reviews": unmatched_reviews,
    "top_categories": top_categories
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

        r'including ([^.]+?)(?: for |\.|$)'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            categories_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and "and", then clean
            categories = re.split(r',|\band\b', categories_text)
            # Clean up each category
            cleaned = []
            for cat in categories:
                cat = cat.strip()
                # Remove leading/trailing whitespace and punctuation
                cat = cat.strip('.')
                if cat and len(cat.strip()) > 0:
                    cleaned.append(cat.strip())
            return cleaned
    
    return []

# Count reviews per category
category_review_count = {}
review_matches = []

for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    # Extract just the number from business_ref
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    
    if business_id_num in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id_num]
        categories = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
        
        if categories:
            review_matches.append({
                'business_ref': business_ref,
                'business_name': business.get('name'),
                'categories': categories
            })
            for category in categories:
                category_review_count[category] = category_review_count.get(category, 0) + 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_5_categories = sorted(category_review_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

result = {
    "total_reviews_from_2016_users": len(reviews_data),
    "reviews_matched_to_businesses": len(review_matches),
    "available_businesses": len(business_data),
    "top_5_categories": top_5_categories
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
